title: Remote Work Impact
description: How has the rise of remote work reshaped urban transportation planning and commuting patterns in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, government reports, and case studies that examine the influence of increased remote work (especially since 2020) on urban transportation systems, traffic volumes, public transit usage, and related planning policies. Relevant material must present empirical data, comparative analyses across multiple cities, and discuss any policy adaptations or infrastructure changes directly linked to the shift toward remote work.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the economic and social impacts of recent renewable energy subsidy programs in Europe?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive analyses of European renewable energy subsidy initiatives introduced in the past five years, with emphasis on measurable economic outcomes (e.g., job creation, investment levels, price effects) and social consequences (e.g., public acceptance, equity, regional development). Relevant documents should describe specific policy mechanisms, provide quantitative or qualitative impact assessments, and distinguish between different EU member states or sectors. Materials focusing solely on technical specifications of renewable technologies, unrelated geographic regions, or purely environmental benefits without economic/social context are not relevant.

title: War Finance Strategies
description: How did the major powers finance their military operations during World War I, and what were the long‑term economic consequences of those financing methods?
narrative: The user is researching the financial mechanisms used by the Allied and Central Powers to fund their war efforts (e.g., war bonds, loans, taxation, and foreign loans) and seeks analysis of how these choices affected post‑war economies, inflation, and debt. Relevant documents should discuss specific financing policies, amounts raised, the role of domestic and international financial markets, and subsequent economic impacts up to the early 1920s.

title: Climate Migration
description: What are the projected numbers of people displaced by climate change by 2050 and which regions will be most affected?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative sources—such as peer‑reviewed studies, UN or World Bank reports, and reputable climate data portals—that quantify expected climate‑induced displacement by the year 2050. Relevant documents must include numeric estimates or ranges, identify the primary geographic hotspots (e.g., low‑lying coasts, Sahel, South Asia), and explain the methodology or scenario assumptions used. Sources that merely discuss climate impacts without displacement figures, anecdotal news stories, or speculative opinion pieces without supporting data are not relevant.

title: Solar Subsidies
description: What federal subsidies are currently available for small‑scale solar photovoltaic installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants current information on government financial incentives, tax credits, or grant programs for residential or small commercial solar PV projects. Relevant documents must detail eligible technologies, qualification criteria, application procedures, monetary amounts, and be published within the past two years.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What strategies have cities implemented to reduce urban heat island effects while preserving green space?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, government reports, or case‑study documents that describe concrete interventions—such as cool roofs, reflective pavements, tree‑canopy expansion, and urban planning policies—used by major metropolitan areas to combat urban heat islands. Relevant results should detail the effectiveness, cost, implementation challenges, and any trade‑offs between temperature reduction and the preservation or enhancement of urban green spaces.

title: Resistance Trends
description: How have rates of antibiotic resistance among community‑acquired bacterial pathogens changed in the United States over the past ten years?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date epidemiological information on the prevalence and temporal trends of antibiotic resistance in common community‑acquired pathogens (e.g., Streptococcus pneumoniae, Staphylococcus aureus, Escherichia coli, Haemophilus influenzae). Relevant documents should present quantitative data (percentages, incidence rates) from surveillance programs, peer‑reviewed studies, or official health agency reports covering roughly the last decade, describe changes over time, and note geographic or demographic patterns. Sources that only discuss mechanisms of resistance without trend data, or that focus on hospital‑acquired infections, are not relevant.

title: AI Explainability Regulations
description: What are the current regulatory requirements for explainability in AI systems deployed in the European Union?
narrative: The user wants a thorough overview of EU legal and policy frameworks that impose explainability or transparency obligations on AI applications. Relevant material must cover the GDPR provisions related to automated decision‑making, the draft EU AI Act and its explainability clauses, any national implementations or guidance documents, and practical compliance examples. Sources should be authoritative (e.g., official legislation, regulator guidance, scholarly analysis) and published after 2020.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user seeks current information on U.S. federal tax incentives for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, specifically the Investment Tax Credit (ITC) amount, eligibility requirements, filing procedures, and any recent legislative updates; only sources that directly address these federal credits for 2024 are relevant, while documents covering state/local programs, unrelated tax benefits, or outdated policies are not.

title: Pacific Fur Trade
description: What were the economic and geopolitical impacts of the Pacific Fur Company’s operations in the early 19th‑century Columbia River region?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles, primary sources, and reputable histories that describe John Jacob Astor’s Pacific Fur Company activities in the Columbia River area, including its trade networks, relationships with Indigenous peoples, competition with other fur traders, and how these factors influenced U.S. and British territorial claims in the Pacific Northwest. Relevant material should focus on the period roughly 1805‑1825 and provide evidence such as trade figures, settlement developments, and diplomatic correspondence.

title: Carbon Capture Funding
description: What federal and state funding programs are available in the United States for carbon capture and storage (CCS) technologies as of 2024?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date, authoritative information on monetary support for CCS projects, including program names, administering agencies, eligibility requirements, award amounts, and application deadlines. Relevant documents should describe U.S. federal initiatives (e.g., DOE’s Carbon Capture Program, EPA’s GHG Reduction Fund) as well as notable state‑level incentives, and should provide enough detail for a researcher or project developer to assess which programs might apply to their technology. Materials focusing on unrelated topics such as cooking or sous‑vide methods are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What strategies and policies have proven effective in reducing urban heat island effects in mid‑size cities?
narrative: The user is seeking evidence‑based approaches—such as green infrastructure, reflective surfaces, and zoning regulations—that have demonstrably lowered ambient temperatures in urban areas with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant documents should describe specific interventions, cite quantitative temperature reductions or health outcomes, and discuss implementation challenges or success factors.

title: Carbon Capture Policies
description: What are the most effective policy mechanisms for incentivizing large‑scale carbon capture and storage (CCS) projects worldwide?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on governmental and regulatory approaches that have successfully promoted CCS, such as subsidies, tax incentives, carbon pricing schemes, permitting frameworks, and international agreements. Relevant documents should detail specific policies, their design, implementation, measurable outcomes, and comparative effectiveness across different countries or regions. Materials that only discuss climate change in general, mention CCS without policy context, or lack concrete examples of policy actions are not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Incentives
description: What federal and state tax incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on financial incentives that can offset the cost of installing solar panels on a home. Relevant documents must describe the federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC) and any applicable state, local, or utility rebate programs, including eligibility criteria, credit amounts or rebate values, application procedures, and deadlines as of the current tax year. Sources that only mention general renewable energy benefits, historical tax credits that are no longer active, or incentives for non‑residential installations are not relevant.

title: Island Renewable Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have proven most effective in accelerating renewable energy deployment in small island developing states?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative sources that analyze and evaluate governmental or institutional policies—such as feed‑in tariffs, net‑metering, renewable portfolio standards, public‑private partnerships, and financing schemes—specifically within small island developing states. Relevant documents should include case studies, empirical assessments, or comparative analyses that demonstrate measurable impacts on renewable energy capacity, affordability, and grid integration. Preference is given to peer‑reviewed literature, reputable reports, or official policy documents published within the last ten years that explicitly address the island context and provide clear criteria for success or lessons learned.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have been most effective at accelerating renewable energy adoption in developing countries?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources—such as academic papers, government reports, and case studies—that evaluate and compare policy tools (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, tax incentives, renewable portfolio standards, and international financing) and their measurable impact on renewable energy deployment in low‑ and middle‑income nations. Relevant documents should discuss outcomes, implementation challenges, and provide quantitative or qualitative evidence of effectiveness.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user seeks current information on U.S. federal financial incentives—specifically tax credits, rebates, or grant programs—applicable to homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant content must detail eligibility requirements, credit percentages or amounts, filing procedures, and any recent legislative updates affecting the program, while excluding commercial, utility‑scale, or non‑U.S. incentives.

title: Renewable Energy Policies
description: What recent policy measures have European Union member states implemented to promote the deployment of renewable energy?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date information on governmental actions within the EU that directly encourage renewable energy projects, such as subsidies, feed‑in tariffs, tax incentives, or regulatory mandates. Relevant documents must describe specific policies adopted after 2020, identify the country or EU body enacting them, and explain how the measures support renewable energy generation or consumption. Sources that merely discuss general climate goals, historical policies before 2020, or non‑EU jurisdictions are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants up-to-date information on financial incentives—such as tax credits, rebates, and grant programs—that apply to homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must identify specific incentive programs, eligibility requirements, monetary values, and effective dates for both the federal level and individual U.S. states. Materials that discuss non‑residential installations, unrelated renewable technologies, or outdated/expired incentive schemes are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives 2024
description: What federal and state incentives are available for small businesses installing solar panels in 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for current (2024) information on financial incentives—including tax credits, rebates, grants, and loan programs—offered by the federal government and individual U.S. states specifically for small businesses (typically fewer than 50 employees) that plan to install solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must detail eligibility requirements, application processes, incentive amounts or percentages, and any relevant deadlines. Materials that focus solely on residential incentives, large‑scale utility projects, or non‑solar renewable technologies are not relevant.

title: Urban Flood Planning
description: What are the most effective strategies for mitigating urban flooding in rapidly growing coastal cities?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on approaches to reduce flood risk in densely populated coastal urban areas. Relevant documents should discuss engineering solutions (e.g., green infrastructure, drainage upgrades), policy and planning frameworks, case studies from comparable cities, and evaluations of effectiveness. Sources that only cover general weather forecasts, unrelated unit conversions, or isolated meteorological phenomena are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Urban
description: How has the rise of remote work influenced urban planning and city infrastructure decisions?
narrative: The user is looking for analyses, studies, or policy documents that discuss the effects of remote work on urban planning, including changes in land use, transportation demand, housing markets, zoning, and municipal services. Relevant material should provide evidence, case studies, or expert commentary on how cities are adapting their planning strategies to accommodate a sustained remote‑work workforce.

title: Dairy-Free Breakfast
description: What are popular, nutritious dairy‑free breakfast foods and meal combinations across Europe?
narrative: The user seeks information about typical breakfast dishes that exclude milk, cheese, and other dairy products, emphasizing nutritional value, cultural variations, and easy preparation for people with dairy intolerance or allergy. Relevant passages should describe specific foods, recipes, portion sizes, and health considerations. Content that focuses on dairy inclusion, non‑European cuisines, or unrelated subjects such as Swiss taxation or mountain tourism is not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: How does the urban heat island effect influence energy consumption in residential buildings during summer months?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly or technical resources that examine the relationship between the urban heat island (UHI) phenomenon and residential energy use in summer, including quantitative measurements, modeling results, and case studies of mitigation measures. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, statistical analyses, or simulation outcomes that link UHI intensity to heating, ventilation, and air‑conditioning loads, discuss geographic or climatic contexts, and evaluate strategies such as green roofs, cool pavements, or urban greening.

title: Renewable Energy Policies
description: What are the most recent national policies promoting renewable energy adoption and how do they differ across major economies?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date information on government initiatives, incentives, and regulations that support renewable energy (e.g., solar, wind, bioenergy) in leading countries such as the United States, Germany, China, and India. Relevant documents should detail policy objectives, implementation dates, fiscal mechanisms (subsidies, tax credits, feed‑in tariffs), and any measurable targets or outcomes associated with these policies.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat islands affect local energy consumption and public health in midsized U.S. cities?
narrative: The user is looking for research, reports, or data that examine the impact of urban heat islands on residential energy use and health outcomes in U.S. cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant material should include quantitative measurements, case studies, mitigation strategies, and comparisons across different climate zones, providing empirical evidence or policy recommendations.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebates are available for residential solar panel installations in 2024?
narrative: The user wants current, detailed information on financial incentives for homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems, including eligibility requirements, credit amounts, application processes, and deadlines. Relevant material must cover the federal Investment Tax Credit, state‑specific programs, local utility rebates, and any recent legislative changes affecting 2024 eligibility.

title: EV Purchase Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are currently available for consumers purchasing a new electric vehicle in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on financial incentives for buying a new electric vehicle, including tax credits, rebates, eligibility requirements, and recent legislative updates at both the federal and state levels. Relevant documents must detail the amounts, qualifying vehicle criteria, application procedures, and any changes to the programs. Materials that only discuss vehicle technology, charging infrastructure, or incentives outside the United States are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: How does the urban heat island effect influence residential energy consumption during summer months?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the relationship between urban heat islands and residential electricity or heating/cooling demand in the summer. Relevant material should include empirical studies, measurement techniques, quantitative analyses of temperature differentials, energy usage statistics, mitigation strategies (e.g., green roofs, reflective materials), and case studies from specific cities. Documents that only mention general climate change without linking it to urban heat islands and residential energy use, or that focus solely on industrial or commercial sectors, are not relevant.

title: Soviet Legacy Tourism
description: What are the major historical sites in former Soviet republics that attract cultural tourists today?
narrative: The user wants current, visitor‑focused information on heritage attractions located within the fifteen former Soviet republics that are directly linked to the Soviet period (e.g., museums, monuments, former government buildings, war memorials). Relevant material must describe each site's location, historical significance, and practical travel details such as opening hours, admission fees, and accessibility. Content that only mentions modern attractions unrelated to Soviet history, or which lacks visitor information, is not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information on U.S. federal tax incentives for homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems, including eligibility criteria, credit amounts, and any recent legislative changes for the current tax year. Relevant documents must describe the 2024‑2025 federal solar tax credit program, qualifying requirements, and filing procedures. Materials that focus on state‑level incentives, historical credit amounts, commercial/industrial installations, or unrelated renewable energy topics are not relevant.

title: LP Registration
description: How does a limited partnership register and conduct business in a state other than the one where it was formed?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on the procedural steps, legal requirements, and practical considerations for a limited partnership (LP) that wishes to expand operations into another U.S. state. Relevant information should cover filing applications for registration, obtaining a certificate of registration, the governance of internal affairs by the formation state’s law, and any compliance or liability implications of operating across state lines.

title: AI Healthcare Workforce
description: How are AI-driven diagnostic tools reshaping the roles and required skills of healthcare professionals in hospitals?
narrative: The user seeks recent scholarly articles, industry reports, and case studies that examine the adoption of AI diagnostic technologies in hospital settings and their impact on staffing, job responsibilities, skill requirements, training programs, and workforce composition. Relevant documents should provide evidence from 2018 onward, include quantitative or qualitative data on staffing changes, discuss emerging skill gaps, and address implications for policy, education, or workforce planning.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What strategies have cities implemented to mitigate urban heat island effects?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on municipal policies, design interventions, and real‑world case studies that demonstrate effective reduction of temperature differentials in dense urban environments. Relevant material should describe specific programs, implementation steps, measured outcomes, and evaluation metrics that illustrate the success or challenges of these mitigation efforts.

title: Celebrity Skin Whitening
description: What products, treatments, or procedures do celebrities use to bleach, whiten, or lighten their skin?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed information about the specific whitening or bleaching methods employed by well‑known public figures, including the names of creams, serums, chemical peels, laser treatments, or other cosmetic procedures they endorse or are reported to use. Relevant documents should mention identified celebrities, describe the products or techniques, cite sources such as interviews, beauty magazines, or reputable news articles, and provide context on how the methods are applied or marketed. General discussions of skin whitening without reference to celebrities or without naming concrete products are not relevant.

title: Urban Green Spaces
description: How do urban green spaces influence mental health and social cohesion in dense city neighborhoods?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and policy-oriented resources that examine the relationship between the presence of parks, community gardens, and other green infrastructure in urban areas and outcomes such as reduced stress, improved well‑being, and stronger neighborhood ties. Relevant documents should provide empirical evidence, theoretical frameworks, or case studies that measure mental‑health benefits or social cohesion indicators, and should focus on dense metropolitan settings rather than suburban or rural contexts.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective at reducing urban heat island effects in mid‑sized cities?
narrative: The user is seeking evidence‑based approaches—such as green roofs, cool pavements, increased tree canopy, and reflective surfaces—demonstrated to lower ambient temperatures in cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant documents should describe the strategy, provide quantitative results (e.g., temperature reduction, energy savings), specify the urban context, and discuss implementation challenges or success factors.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks current, detailed information on financial incentives for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, including federal tax credits (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit), state and local rebates, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and any recent legislative updates. Documents are relevant if they describe these programs, their monetary values, qualifying criteria, and how to claim them.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the main challenges European countries encounter when implementing renewable energy subsidy programs?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources that discuss policy obstacles, economic and regulatory barriers, and real‑world case studies related to renewable energy subsidies in Europe. Relevant documents should describe specific challenges, provide analysis of their causes, and possibly suggest solutions or best practices within the European context.

title: Residential Solar Incentives
description: What recent policies encourage homeowners in the United States to install solar photovoltaic systems?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date (2023‑2025) federal, state, or local policy mechanisms—such as tax credits, rebates, net‑metering rules, or financing programs—that specifically target residential solar adoption. Relevant material must describe the incentive details, eligibility requirements, monetary values, and implementation dates. General renewable‑energy discussions that do not focus on homeowner solar incentives are not relevant.

title: Battery Storage Policy
description: What are the main challenges and proposed solutions for integrating large‑scale battery storage into national electricity grids by 2030?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on technical, regulatory, and economic obstacles to nationwide deployment of grid‑scale battery storage, as well as documented policy recommendations and case studies that address these challenges. Relevant documents should discuss integration strategies, cost‑benefit analyses, regulatory frameworks, and real‑world implementation examples from multiple countries.

title: Celtic Deities
description: What are the principal deities in Celtic mythology and the core stories associated with each?
narrative: The user seeks a scholarly overview of the most important Celtic gods and goddesses, including their attributes, mythic narratives, and historical sources. Relevant documents should contain academic or reputable mythological references, provide the names and roles of the deities, and describe at least one traditional legend per deity. Works that merely mention Celtic culture without focusing on deities, or modern fantasy adaptations, are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What are the most effective financial incentives for residential solar panel adoption in the United States between 2015 and 2023?
narrative: The user seeks documents that describe, compare, and evaluate federal, state, and local monetary programs—such as tax credits, rebates, and low‑interest loans—that have demonstrably increased residential solar installations. Relevant material should focus on the U.S., cover the 2015‑2023 period, and provide quantitative or qualitative evidence of each incentive's impact, including adoption rates, cost‑benefit analyses, or case studies.
